metadata.form_title: Schedule 8812 (Form 1040) — Credits for Qualifying Children and Other Dependents

facts.label: Dependent age at end of tax year
facts.notes: Per-Dependent fact — repeated per row in the Dependents table. CTC requires <17; ODC has no age requirement.

facts.label: Dependent relationship to taxpayer
facts.notes: Per-Dependent fact. CTC qualifying-child test fails if 'other'.

facts.label: Months dependent resided with taxpayer
facts.notes: Per-Dependent fact. 0-12. CTC requires >6. §152(c)(4) tie-breaker resolved upstream.

facts.label: Did dependent provide over ½ own support?
facts.notes: Per-Dependent fact. If true, fails §152(c)(1)(D).

facts.label: Did dependent file a joint return?
facts.notes: Per-Dependent fact. If true (except solely for refund — pre-filtered upstream), fails §152(c)(1)(E).

facts.label: Dependent citizenship status
facts.notes: Per-Dependent fact. Per §152(b)(3). CTC/ODC require US cit/nat/res-alien.

facts.label: Dependent TIN type
facts.notes: Per-Dependent fact. valid_ssn = work-authorized SSN issued before due date. CTC requires valid_ssn; ODC accepts valid_ssn/itin/atin.

facts.label: Is this person claimed as a dependent on the return?
facts.notes: Per-Dependent fact. Per §152(a). Tie-breaker rules resolved upstream.

facts.label: Is dependent permanently and totally disabled?
facts.notes: Per-Dependent fact. Affects ODC age tests (not CTC — CTC has hard <17).

facts.label: Filing status
facts.notes: Return-level fact. Drives phaseout threshold.

facts.label: Does taxpayer have a valid SSN?
facts.notes: Return-level fact. Per OBBBA §70104(b). NEW for TY 2025.

facts.label: Does spouse have a valid SSN (MFJ only)?
facts.notes: Return-level fact. Material only if filing_status = MFJ.

facts.label: Does spouse have SSN or ITIN by due date (MFJ only)?
facts.notes: Return-level fact. MFJ filing requirement when taxpayer has SSN but spouse doesn't.

facts.label: Form 1040 Line 11 AGI
facts.notes: Return-level fact. Schedule 8812 Line 1 input.

facts.label: Puerto Rico excluded income
facts.notes: Return-level fact. Schedule 8812 Line 2a.

facts.label: Form 2555 excluded amount (lines 45 + 50 combined)
facts.notes: Return-level fact. Schedule 8812 Line 2b. Combined per design decision Q4 (single addback).

facts.label: Form 4563 line 15 — American Samoa exclusion
facts.notes: Return-level fact. Schedule 8812 Line 2c.

facts.label: Does taxpayer file Form 2555?
facts.notes: Return-level fact. Disqualifies ACTC entirely; CTC and ODC remain available.

facts.label: Earned income for ACTC (per Earned Income Worksheet)
facts.notes: Return-level fact. Schedule 8812 Line 18a. Computed in tts-tax-app per the Earned Income Worksheet (Sch 8812 Instructions p. 8): wages + combat pay (elected) + statutory + Sch C/F/K-1 net − ½ SE tax. DEFERRED WORK: a future spec session will decompose the worksheet to serve both ACTC and EIC computations.

facts.label: Nontaxable combat pay (Line 18b)
facts.notes: Return-level fact. Schedule 8812 Line 18b. Election: include in EI for ACTC.

facts.label: Tax before CTC (Form 1040 Line 18)
facts.notes: Return-level fact. Regular tax + AMT + Sch 2 line 3.

facts.label: Schedule 3 credits applied before CTC (sum of lines 1, 2, 3, 4, 5b, 6d, 6f, 6l, 6m)
facts.notes: Return-level fact. Per Credit Limit Worksheet A.

facts.label: Withheld SS + Medicare taxes (W-2 box 4 + box 6, both spouses if MFJ)
facts.notes: Return-level fact. Schedule 8812 Line 21 (Part II-B).

facts.label: Additional Medicare Tax (Form 8959 line 7)
facts.notes: Return-level fact. Part II-B refinement deferred — see D010.

facts.label: ½ SE tax deduction (Schedule 1 line 15)
facts.notes: Return-level fact. Part of Schedule 8812 Line 22.

facts.label: SE tax total (Schedule 2 line 5)
facts.notes: Return-level fact. Part of Schedule 8812 Line 22.

facts.label: Unreported SS/Medicare tax (Schedule 2 line 6, from Forms 4137/8919)
facts.notes: Return-level fact. Part of Schedule 8812 Line 22.

facts.label: Other employment taxes (Schedule 2 line 13)
facts.notes: Return-level fact. Part of Schedule 8812 Line 22.

facts.label: EITC claimed (Form 1040 Line 27a)
facts.notes: Return-level fact. Schedule 8812 Line 24 component.

facts.label: Excess SS/RRTA withheld (Schedule 3 line 11)
facts.notes: Return-level fact. Schedule 8812 Line 24 component.

facts.label: Claims credits requiring Worksheet B? (Form 8396/8839/5695 Part I/8859)
facts.notes: Return-level fact. Triggers D009. Preparer-set flag.

facts.label: Taxpayer has Tier 1 RRTA taxes (W-2 box 14 or CT-2)?
facts.notes: Return-level fact. Triggers D011. Part II-B RRTA handling deferred.

facts.label: Modified AGI (Schedule 8812 Line 3)
facts.notes: Calculated. agi_line_11 + addbacks.

facts.label: Count of qualifying children for CTC (Line 4)
facts.notes: Calculated by aggregation over Dependents where dep_qualifies_ctc = True.

facts.label: Count of other dependents for ODC (Line 6)
facts.notes: Calculated by aggregation over Dependents where dep_qualifies_odc = True.

facts.label: Return is SSN-eligible for CTC/ACTC?
facts.notes: Calculated per OBBBA §70104(b). For MFJ: at least one spouse SSN. For others: taxpayer SSN.

facts.label: Eligible for ACTC?
facts.notes: Calculated. False if Form 2555 filer OR taxpayer SSN missing OR no QC OR Line 12 = 0.

facts.label: Part II-B alternate ACTC triggered?
facts.notes: Calculated. True if count_qc >= 3 AND L_20 < L_17.

rules.title: Dependent qualifies for CTC (7-test classification)
rules.description: Iteration semantics: ONCE PER DEPENDENT. Tests all seven CTC qualifying-child conditions per §24(c) + §152(c) + §24(h)(7) SSN. Tie-breaker rules (§152(c)(4)) and joint-return-only-for-refund exception assumed resolved upstream.
rules.formula: dep_qualifies_ctc = (dep_age_at_eoy < 17 AND dep_relationship_code != 'other' AND dep_months_resided_with_taxpayer > 6 AND NOT dep_provided_over_half_own_support AND NOT dep_filed_joint_return AND dep_citizenship_status IN ('us_citizen','us_national','us_resident_alien') AND dep_tin_type == 'valid_ssn' AND dep_is_claimed_as_dependent)

authorities.title: IRC §24 — Child Tax Credit and Credit for Other Dependents
IRC §152 — Dependent Defined
2025 Instructions for Schedule 8812 (Form 1040) — Credits for Qualifying Children and Other Dependents
Public Law 119-21 §70104 — Extension and Enhancement of Increased Child Tax Credit (One Big Beautiful Bill Act)

rules.title: Dependent qualifies for ODC
rules.description: Iteration semantics: ONCE PER DEPENDENT. Per §24(h)(4). Note: a QC without SSN drops to ODC if has ITIN/ATIN (per Schedule 8812 Form Line 6 caution).
rules.formula: dep_qualifies_odc = (dep_is_claimed_as_dependent AND NOT dep_qualifies_ctc AND dep_citizenship_status IN ('us_citizen','us_national','us_resident_alien') AND dep_tin_type IN ('valid_ssn','itin','atin'))

rules.description: Iteration semantics: ONCE PER RETURN. NEW for TY 2025. For non-MFJ: taxpayer must have valid SSN. For MFJ: at least one spouse SSN sufficient (other needs SSN or ITIN by due date).

rules.description: Iteration semantics: ONCE PER RETURN. Sch 8812 Line 9. $400K MFJ; $200K for Single, HOH, MFS, QSS. Per §24(h)(3) override of §24(b)(2) — made permanent by OBBBA §70104(a)(1).

rules.title: Phaseout excess (Line 10) — round UP to next $1,000
rules.description: Iteration semantics: ONCE PER RETURN. Sch 8812 Line 10. CRITICAL: any non-zero excess rounds UP to next $1,000 — even $1 of excess yields $1,000 on Line 10. Implements §24(b)(1) 'or fraction thereof' rule. Common bug: floor instead of ceil.

rules.description: Iteration semantics: ONCE PER RETURN. Sch 8812 Line 11. Equivalent to '$50 per $1,000' from §24(b)(1).

rules.title: Net credit post-phaseout (Line 12)
rules.description: Iteration semantics: ONCE PER RETURN. Sch 8812 Line 12. If L_8 ≤ L_11, STOP — no CTC, ODC, or ACTC.
rules.formula: L_12 = max(0, L_8 - L_11) if L_8 > L_11 else 0  -- STOP if L_8 <= L_11

rules.description: Iteration semantics: ONCE PER RETURN. Sch 8812 Line 13. Credit Limit Worksheet A: Form 1040 Line 18 minus Schedule 3 lines 1, 2, 3, 4, 5b, 6d, 6f, 6l, 6m. Worksheet B applies when other competing credits exist — see D009 diagnostic.

rules.description: Iteration semantics: ONCE PER RETURN. Form 2555 caution + return SSN gate + QC required.
rules.formula: actc_eligible = (L_12 > 0 AND NOT files_form_2555 AND return_ssn_eligible_for_ctc_actc AND count_qualifying_children > 0)

rules.title: Force zero if taxpayer SSN missing (return-level override)
rules.description: Iteration semantics: ONCE PER RETURN. HIGHEST PRECEDENCE override per OBBBA §70104(b). Forces both nonrefundable (L_14) and refundable (L_27) to $0 when taxpayer (and spouse if MFJ) lacks valid SSN. Verbatim STOP language: 'If you, and your spouse if filing jointly, do not have a valid SSN, you can't claim the CTC or ACTC.'
rules.formula: IF NOT return_ssn_eligible_for_ctc_actc THEN L_14 = 0 AND L_27 = 0

authorities.relevance_note: Sch 8812 Instructions E12 — STOP language verbatim

line_map.description: Number of qualifying children under 17 with valid SSN
Number of other dependents (includes QC w/o SSN if has ITIN/ATIN)
Excess of MAGI over threshold (rounded UP to $1,000)
Net credit post-phaseout (Line 8 − Line 11). STOP if ≤ 0
Tax liability cap (Credit Limit Worksheet A)
CTC + ODC nonrefundable (min of Line 12, Line 13)
Earned income excess (Line 18a − $2,500, floored at 0). NOTE: This is Sch 8812 Line 19, NOT Form 1040 Line 19.
SS + Medicare + Additional Medicare taxes (W-2 box 4 + 6, both spouses if MFJ)

diagnostics.title: Dependent would qualify for CTC but missing SSN
diagnostics.condition: PER-DEP: dep_is_claimed_as_dependent AND dep_age_at_eoy < 17 AND dep_relationship_code != 'other' AND dep_months_resided_with_taxpayer > 6 AND NOT dep_provided_over_half_own_support AND NOT dep_filed_joint_return AND dep_citizenship_status IN ('us_citizen','us_national','us_resident_alien') AND dep_tin_type != 'valid_ssn'
diagnostics.message: Dependent meets all qualifying-child tests for CTC except the valid-SSN requirement. CTC of $2,200 is NOT available. ODC of $500 may be available if the dependent has ITIN or ATIN.

diagnostics.title: Taxpayer SSN missing — entire CTC/ACTC/ODC unavailable
diagnostics.message: Return cannot claim CTC, ACTC, or ODC because taxpayer (and spouse if MFJ) lacks valid SSN. New requirement for TY 2025 per OBBBA §70104(b). ODC also unavailable if neither has SSN or ITIN.

diagnostics.title: MAGI within $25K of phaseout threshold
diagnostics.condition: RETURN: NOT D013_fired AND ((filing_status == 'MFJ' AND ABS(magi - 400000) <= 25000) OR (filing_status != 'MFJ' AND ABS(magi - 200000) <= 25000))
diagnostics.message: MAGI is within $25,000 of the CTC phaseout threshold. Verify MAGI computation including AGI plus Form 2555 lines 45+50, Puerto Rico, American Samoa add-backs.

diagnostics.title: ACTC limited by 15% earned-income method (not per-child cap)
diagnostics.condition: RETURN: actc_eligible AND L_27 > 0 AND L_27 < L_16b
diagnostics.message: Refundable ACTC is below the per-child maximum because the 15% earned-income method is binding. Verify earned income on Sch 8812 Line 18a includes all eligible items.

diagnostics.title: 3+ qualifying children — verify Part II-B path
diagnostics.message: Return has 3+ qualifying children. Schedule 8812 Part II-B alternate ACTC computation may yield a larger refundable credit than the 15% method (common in SE returns without EITC). Verify Part II-B was evaluated.

diagnostics.title: Form 2555 filer — ACTC disqualified, CTC/ODC still allowed
diagnostics.message: Taxpayer files Form 2555 (foreign earned income exclusion). ACTC is NOT available (Sch 8812 Part II-A caution); CTC and ODC remain available with MAGI add-back via Line 2b.

diagnostics.title: Tie-breaker reminder (shared-custody)
diagnostics.condition: RETURN: filing_status IN ('Single','HOH','MFS') AND count_qualifying_children > 0 AND ANY(dep_relationship_code IN ('child','adopted_child','foster_child','descendant_of_child'))
diagnostics.message: Tie-breaker reminder: confirm §152(c)(4) was applied if any qualifying child has multiple potential claimants (divorced parents, non-parent guardians, etc.). Parent prevails over non-parent; between two parents, longer residency then higher AGI.

diagnostics.title: Dependent aged 17+ — CTC unavailable, ODC may apply
diagnostics.condition: PER-DEP: dep_age_at_eoy >= 17 AND dep_is_claimed_as_dependent AND dep_relationship_code IN ('child','adopted_child','foster_child','descendant_of_child') AND dep_tin_type != 'none'
diagnostics.message: Dependent is age 17 or older. CTC requires under 17 per §24(c). Dependent qualifies for ODC ($500) if otherwise eligible. Common preparer error in the year a child turns 17.

diagnostics.title: Worksheet B applicability (other credits compete)
diagnostics.condition: RETURN: claims_credits_requiring_worksheet_b == True AND (count_qualifying_children > 0 OR count_other_dependents > 0) AND NOT files_form_2555
diagnostics.message: Taxpayer claims other credits (Form 8396, 8839, 5695 Part I, or 8859) that interact with CTC for tax-liability ordering. Credit Limit Worksheet B applies instead of Worksheet A. Rule Studio v1 models Worksheet A only — Worksheet B deferred until Schedule 3 credits are specced.

diagnostics.title: Additional Medicare Tax — Part II-B Line 21 may need refinement
diagnostics.message: Taxpayer paid Additional Medicare Tax (Form 8959). Sch 8812 Part II-B Line 21 requires the Additional Medicare Tax and RRTA Tax Worksheet to avoid double-counting. Rule Studio v1 uses the common case formula — verify against Form 8959 if amounts diverge.

diagnostics.title: RRTA taxes present — Part II-B not modeled
diagnostics.condition: RETURN: taxpayer_has_rrta_taxes == True AND actc_part_iib_triggered == True
diagnostics.message: Taxpayer has Tier 1 RRTA taxes (railroad employee/representative). Sch 8812 Part II-B has RRTA-specific provisions not modeled in Rule Studio v1. Compute Line 21 manually using the Additional Medicare Tax and RRTA Tax Worksheet and override Line 27.

diagnostics.title: Phaseout zeroed the credit (STOP at Line 12)
diagnostics.message: Phaseout reduction (Line 11) equals or exceeds the combined credit (Line 8). Per Sch 8812 Line 12: STOP — no CTC, ODC, or ACTC available. This is the expected result for high-income taxpayers; verify MAGI inputs if unexpected.

tests.scenario_name: TS01 — MFJ, 2 QC, no phaseout, ample tax liability
TS02 — MFJ, 2 QC, low tax → ACTC spillover at per-child cap
TS03 — Single, 1 QC + 1 ODC, mixed credits
TS04 — MFJ, 1 QC, $410K AGI → exact-$1,000 excess phaseout
TS05 — MFJ, 1 QC, $410,500 AGI → 'fraction thereof' rounding test
TS05b — MFJ, 1 QC, $400,001 AGI → minimum positive phaseout
TS06 — MFJ, 1 QC, $450K AGI → phaseout exceeds credit; STOP
TS07 — HOH, 2 QC, very low EI → small ACTC via 15% method
TS08 — HOH, 2 QC, $30K EI → ACTC at per-child cap
TS09 — MFJ, 1 dep ITIN-only, falls back to ODC
TS09b — ODC only with TLC=$200 — validates ODC NOT refundable
TS10 — Single, no taxpayer SSN, 1 QC w/SSN → all credits zero
TS11 — MFJ, both spouses no SSN, 2 QC → all credits zero
TS12 — MFS, 1 QC, $250K AGI → MFS threshold $200K binds; STOP
TS13 — MFJ files Form 2555, 2 QC → CTC OK, ACTC blocked
TS14 — HOH, 3 QC, $25K SE, no EITC → Part II-B beats 15% method
TS14b — HOH, 3 QC, $15K W-2, EITC swamps Part II-B
TS_WSB_TBD — Worksheet B test scenarios deferred

excerpts.excerpt_text: For purposes of this subtitle, the term 'dependent' means— (1) a qualifying child, or (2) a qualifying relative.
excerpts.summary_text: Dependent = qualifying child OR qualifying relative.

excerpts.excerpt_text: The term 'dependent' does not include an individual who is not a citizen or national of the United States unless such individual is a resident of the United States or a country contiguous to the United States.
excerpts.summary_text: Dependents must be US citizen, US national, or US resident alien (or resident of Canada/Mexico).

excerpts.excerpt_text: The term 'qualifying child' means ... an individual— (A) who bears a relationship to the taxpayer described in paragraph (2), (B) who has the same principal place of abode as the taxpayer for more than one-half of such taxable year, (C) who meets the age requirements of paragraph (3), (D) who has not provided over one-half of such individual's own support for the calendar year ..., and (E) who has not filed a joint return ... with the individual's spouse under section 6013 for the taxable year ....
excerpts.summary_text: Five tests: relationship, residency (>½ year), age, support, joint return.

excerpts.excerpt_text: For purposes of paragraph (1)(A), an individual bears a relationship to the taxpayer described in this paragraph if such individual is— (A) a child of the taxpayer or a descendant of such a child, or (B) a brother, sister, stepbrother, or stepsister of the taxpayer or a descendant of any such relative.
excerpts.summary_text: Relationship: child/descendant OR sibling/descendant. §152(f)(1)(B) extends 'child' to adopted, foster, step.

excerpts.excerpt_text: If multiple taxpayers may claim the same qualifying child, the child is treated as the qualifying child of: (i) a parent if any parent could claim, or (ii) the taxpayer with the highest adjusted gross income. For two parents not filing jointly: (i) parent with longest residency, then (ii) higher-AGI parent. A non-parent claimant must have AGI higher than any parent's.
excerpts.summary_text: Tie-breaker order: parent > non-parent; between two parents, longer residency then higher AGI.

authority_sources.title: IRC §24 — Child Tax Credit and Credit for Other Dependents

excerpts.excerpt_text: There shall be allowed as a credit against the tax imposed by this chapter for the taxable year with respect to each qualifying child of the taxpayer for which the taxpayer is allowed a deduction under section 151 an amount equal to $1,000.
excerpts.summary_text: Base statutory amount $1,000; superseded by §24(h)(2) for 2018+.

excerpts.excerpt_text: The amount of the credit allowable under subsection (a) shall be reduced (but not below zero) by $50 for each $1,000 (or fraction thereof) by which the taxpayer's modified adjusted gross income exceeds the threshold amount.
excerpts.summary_text: $50 per $1,000 of MAGI over threshold; round UP for partial $1,000.

excerpts.excerpt_text: Base §24(b)(2) thresholds are $110,000 (MFJ) / $75,000 (single) / $55,000 (MFS). For tax years beginning after 2017, §24(h)(3) OVERRIDES with: '$400,000 in the case of a joint return ($200,000 in any other case).' OBBBA §70104(a)(1) removed the §24(h)(1) sunset, making this override permanent.
excerpts.summary_text: Operational thresholds for TY 2025: $400K MFJ / $200K all other (per §24(h)(3), made permanent by OBBBA).

excerpts.excerpt_text: The term 'qualifying child' means a qualifying child of the taxpayer (as defined in section 152(c)) who has not attained age 17.
excerpts.summary_text: §24(c) under-17 age modifier overrides §152(c)(3)'s general age tests.

excerpts.excerpt_text: §24(d)(1)(B)(i) provides the refundable portion equals 15% of earned income in excess of $3,000. §24(h)(6) OVERRIDES the $3,000 figure to $2,500 for tax years beginning after 2017. OBBBA §70104(a)(1) made this override permanent.
excerpts.summary_text: Operational earned-income floor for TY 2025: $2,500 (per §24(h)(6)). 15% rate above floor.

excerpts.excerpt_text: (2) CREDIT AMOUNT.—Subsection (a) shall be applied by substituting '$2,200' for '$1,000'.
excerpts.summary_text: Per-child CTC amount $2,200, eff. TY 2025+ per OBBBA §70104(a)(2).

excerpts.excerpt_text: Subsection (a) shall be applied by substituting '$500' for '$1,000' ... with respect to any dependent of the taxpayer (as defined in section 152) other than a qualifying child described in subsection (c).
excerpts.summary_text: $500 ODC per non-QC dependent. Not amended by OBBBA.

excerpts.excerpt_text: MAXIMUM AMOUNT OF REFUNDABLE CREDIT.—The amount determined under subsection (d)(1)(A) with respect to any qualifying child shall not exceed $1,400. Per §24(i)(1) as amended by OBBBA §70104(c), this $1,400 is COLA-adjusted starting 2025 (base year 2017), rounded down to nearest $100. TY 2025 indexed value = $1,700.
excerpts.summary_text: ACTC per-child refundable cap for TY 2025: $1,700 (= $1,400 base indexed to 2025 dollars).

excerpts.excerpt_text: No credit shall be allowed under this section to a taxpayer with respect to any qualifying child unless the taxpayer includes on the return of tax for the taxable year— (i) the taxpayer's social security number (or, in the case of a joint return, the social security number of at least 1 spouse), and (ii) the social security number of such qualifying child.
excerpts.summary_text: New for TY 2025: taxpayer SSN required (MFJ: at least one spouse). Child SSN required.

authority_sources.title: Instructions for Form 1040 — U.S. Individual Income Tax Return

excerpts.excerpt_label: Adjustments to income and standard deduction
excerpts.excerpt_text: Line 10: Adjustments to income from Schedule 1 Part II — includes educator expenses ($300), HSA deduction, self-employment tax deduction (50%), self-employed SEP/SIMPLE/qualified plans, self-employed health insurance deduction, penalty on early withdrawal of savings, IRA deduction, student loan interest deduction ($2,500 max), and other above-the-line deductions. Line 11: Adjusted gross income (AGI). Line 12: Standard deduction or itemized deductions (Schedule A). Standard deduction for 2025: $15,750 Single/MFS, $31,500 MFJ/QSS, $23,625 HOH. Additional standard deduction for age 65+ or blind: $1,600 (married) / $2,000 (unmarried).
excerpts.summary_text: Above-the-line: educator, HSA, SE tax, IRA, student loan. Std deduction $15,750/$31,500/$23,625 (2025).

excerpts.excerpt_text: Five filing statuses: (1) Single — unmarried or legally separated on Dec 31. (2) Married Filing Jointly (MFJ) — both spouses report all income; both are jointly and severally liable. (3) Married Filing Separately (MFS) — may result in higher tax; certain credits/deductions are reduced or disallowed. (4) Head of Household (HOH) — unmarried on Dec 31, paid more than half the cost of keeping up a home for a qualifying person. (5) Qualifying Surviving Spouse — spouse died in one of the two prior tax years, has a dependent child, and has not remarried. Filing status determines: standard deduction amount, tax bracket thresholds, eligibility for credits and deductions.
excerpts.summary_text: 5 filing statuses: Single, MFJ, MFS, HOH, QSS. Determines brackets, std deduction, credit eligibility.

excerpts.excerpt_text: Line 1: Wages, salaries, tips (from W-2). Line 2a: Tax-exempt interest. Line 2b: Taxable interest (Schedule B if > $1,500). Line 3a: Qualified dividends. Line 3b: Ordinary dividends (Schedule B if > $1,500). Line 4a: IRA distributions. Line 4b: Taxable IRA distributions. Line 5a: Pensions and annuities. Line 5b: Taxable pensions. Line 6a: Social Security benefits. Line 6b: Taxable Social Security (up to 85% may be taxable). Line 7: Capital gain or loss (Schedule D, or directly if only Form 1099-B with no adjustments). Line 8: Other income from Schedule 1 (business income, rental, farm, unemployment, alimony received pre-2019, gambling, etc.). Line 9: Total income.
excerpts.summary_text: Income: wages, interest, dividends, IRA/pensions, Social Security, capital gains, Sch 1 other income.

excerpts.excerpt_text: Line 19. Child tax credit and credit for other dependents. To claim the credit, see the Instructions for Schedule 8812. Enter on line 19 the amount from Schedule 8812, line 14.
excerpts.summary_text: Form 1040 Line 19 = Schedule 8812 Line 14.

excerpts.excerpt_text: Line 28. Additional child tax credit. Use Schedule 8812 to figure the credit. Enter on line 28 the amount from Schedule 8812, line 27.
excerpts.summary_text: Form 1040 Line 28 = Schedule 8812 Line 27.

excerpts.excerpt_text: Line 13: Qualified business income deduction (§199A) — 20% of QBI from Form 8995 or 8995-A. Line 14: Total deductions. Line 15: Taxable income. Line 16: Tax — from Tax Table, Tax Computation Worksheet, or Qualified Dividends and Capital Gain Tax Worksheet (preferential rates for LTCG and qualified dividends). Schedule 2 Part I: AMT (Form 6251), excess premium tax credit repayment. Line 19: Child tax credit ($2,200 per qualifying child per OBBBA, up from $2,000) / credit for other dependents (Schedule 8812). Line 21: Total credits from Schedule 3 — foreign tax credit, education credits (Form 8863), retirement savings credit, child/dependent care credit (Form 2441), energy credits, and other nonrefundable credits.
excerpts.summary_text: Tax on taxable income (brackets or cap gains rates). Credits: child tax, education, foreign tax, energy.

authority_sources.title: 2025 Schedule 8812 (Form 1040) — Credits for Qualifying Children and Other Dependents

excerpts.excerpt_text: 1 Enter the amount from line 11a of your Form 1040, 1040-SR, or 1040-NR. 2a Enter income from Puerto Rico that you excluded. 2b Enter the amounts from lines 45 and 50 of your Form 2555. 2c Enter the amount from line 15 of your Form 4563. 2d Add lines 2a through 2c. 3 Add lines 1 and 2d.
excerpts.summary_text: Line 3 = MAGI = AGI + (PR excluded + Form 2555 lines 45+50 + Form 4563 line 15).

excerpts.excerpt_label: Part I — Lines 12-14 nonrefundable cap; Form 1040 Line 19 destination
excerpts.excerpt_text: 12 Is the amount on line 8 more than the amount on line 11? No. Stop here. You cannot take the child tax credit, credit for other dependents, or additional child tax credit. Yes. Subtract line 11 from line 8. Enter the result. 13 Enter the amount from Credit Limit Worksheet A. 14 Enter the smaller of line 12 or line 13. This is your child tax credit and credit for other dependents. Enter this amount on Form 1040, 1040-SR, or 1040-NR, line 19.
excerpts.summary_text: Line 14 = min(Line 12, Line 13) → Form 1040 Line 19. STOP if Line 8 ≤ Line 11.

excerpts.excerpt_text: 4 Number of qualifying children under age 17 with the required social security number. 5 Multiply line 4 by $2,200. 6 Number of other dependents, including any qualifying children who are not under age 17 or who do not have the required social security number. Caution: Do not include yourself, your spouse, or anyone who is not a U.S. citizen, U.S. national, or U.S. resident alien. Also, do not include anyone you included on line 4. 7 Multiply line 6 by $500. 8 Add lines 5 and 7.
excerpts.summary_text: Lines 4-8: QC count × $2,200 + ODC count × $500 = pre-phaseout combined credit.

excerpts.excerpt_label: Part I — Lines 9-11 phaseout with ceil-rounding
excerpts.excerpt_text: 9 Enter the amount shown below for your filing status. Married filing jointly—$400,000; All other filing statuses—$200,000. 10 Subtract line 9 from line 3. If zero or less, enter -0-. If more than zero and not a multiple of $1,000, enter the next multiple of $1,000. For example, if the result is $425, enter $1,000; if the result is $1,025, enter $2,000, etc. 11 Multiply line 10 by 5% (0.05).
excerpts.summary_text: Threshold $400K MFJ / $200K all other; excess rounded UP to next $1,000; reduction = 5% × Line 10.

excerpts.excerpt_label: Part II-A — Lines 15-20 ACTC base computation
excerpts.excerpt_text: Caution: If you file Form 2555, you cannot claim the additional child tax credit. 15 Reserved for future use. 16a Subtract line 14 from line 12. 16b Number of qualifying children under age 17 with the required social security number: × $1,700. 17 Enter the smaller of line 16a or line 16b. 18a Earned income (see instructions). 18b Nontaxable combat pay. 19 Is the amount on line 18a more than $2,500? Yes. Subtract $2,500 from the amount on line 18a. 20 Multiply the amount on line 19 by 15% (0.15).
excerpts.summary_text: ACTC base: per-child cap $1,700; earned-income floor $2,500; 15% method. Form 2555 disqualifies.

excerpts.excerpt_label: Part II-B + II-C — Lines 21-27 alternate path and ACTC destination
excerpts.excerpt_text: 21 Withheld social security, Medicare, and Additional Medicare taxes from Form(s) W-2, boxes 4 and 6. 22 Schedule 1 line 15 + Schedule 2 lines 5, 6, and 13. 23 Add lines 21 and 22. 24 Form 1040/1040-SR line 27a + Schedule 3 line 11. 25 Subtract line 24 from line 23. If zero or less, enter -0-. 26 Enter the larger of line 20 or line 25. 27 This is your additional child tax credit. Enter this amount on Form 1040, 1040-SR, or 1040-NR, line 28.
excerpts.summary_text: Part II-B (3+ QC alternate path): payroll-tax floor for ACTC. Line 27 → Form 1040 Line 28.

authority_sources.title: 2025 Instructions for Schedule 8812 (Form 1040) — Credits for Qualifying Children and Other Dependents

excerpts.excerpt_text: CTC amount increased. The maximum amount of CTC for each qualifying child increased to $2,200.
excerpts.summary_text: Per-QC CTC = $2,200 (TY 2025).

excerpts.excerpt_text: Earned income = wages (Form 1040 line 1z) + nontaxable combat pay (elected, Line 18b) + statutory employee income + Schedule C net profit + Schedule K-1 (1065) box 14 code A (other than farming) + Schedule F net farm profit + elected Medicaid waiver payments; minus ½ SE tax deduction (Schedule 1 line 15). Income excluded under a tax treaty is also excluded from earned income on line 18a. For bona fide PR residents: do not include income earned in Puerto Rico.
excerpts.summary_text: EI = wages + combat pay (elected) + statutory + Sch C/F/K-1 — ½ SE tax. Excl: tax-treaty, PR.

excerpts.excerpt_text: If you erroneously claim the CTC, ACTC, or ODC and it is later determined that your error was due to reckless or intentional disregard of the CTC, ACTC, or ODC rules, you will not be allowed to claim any of these credits for 2 years even if you are otherwise eligible to do so. If it is determined that your error was due to fraud, you will not be allowed to claim any of these credits for 10 years.
excerpts.summary_text: Reckless disregard: 2-year bar. Fraud: 10-year bar. Form 8862 required after prior disallowance.

excerpts.excerpt_label: E12 — STOP language for taxpayer-SSN failure
excerpts.excerpt_text: If you, and your spouse if filing jointly, do not have a valid SSN, you can't claim the CTC or ACTC on either your original or an amended 2025 return.
excerpts.summary_text: Verbatim STOP language for return-level taxpayer-SSN disqualifier.

excerpts.excerpt_text: ACTC amount. The maximum amount of ACTC for each qualifying child is $1,700.
excerpts.summary_text: Per-QC ACTC refundable cap = $1,700 (TY 2025).

excerpts.excerpt_label: E3 — What's New: Taxpayer SSN requirement
excerpts.excerpt_text: Social security number (SSN) required to claim the child tax credit (CTC) and additional child tax credit (ACTC). Beginning in tax year 2025, you must have a valid SSN to claim the CTC or ACTC. If you are filing a joint return, only one filer must have a valid SSN. The other filer must have either an SSN or an individual taxpayer identification number (ITIN), and it must have been issued on or before the due date of your return.
excerpts.summary_text: TY 2025+: taxpayer needs valid SSN. MFJ: only one spouse SSN required (other needs SSN or ITIN).

excerpts.excerpt_text: Valid SSN. For the CTC and ACTC, a valid SSN is one that is valid for employment and that is issued by the Social Security Administration before the due date of your 2025 return (including extensions).
excerpts.summary_text: Valid SSN = work-authorized, issued by extended due date.

excerpts.excerpt_text: To claim a child for the CTC and ACTC, the child must be your dependent, under age 17 at the end of 2025, and meet all the conditions in Steps 1 through 3 under Who Qualifies as Your Dependent in the Instructions for Form 1040.
excerpts.summary_text: CTC/ACTC: child must be dependent, under 17, meet 1040 Steps 1-3 (i.e., §152(c)).

excerpts.excerpt_text: The ODC is for individuals with a dependent who meets the following conditions. 1. The person is claimed as a dependent on your return. 2. The person can't be used by you to claim the CTC or ACTC. 3. The person was a U.S. citizen, U.S. national, or U.S. resident alien. 4. The person has an SSN, ITIN, or ATIN issued on or before the due date of your return (including extensions).
excerpts.summary_text: ODC: dependent, NOT a QC for CTC, US cit/nat/res-alien, has SSN/ITIN/ATIN by due date.

excerpts.excerpt_text: Modified AGI. For purposes of the CTC and ODC, your modified AGI is the amount on line 3 of Schedule 8812.
excerpts.summary_text: MAGI = Sch 8812 Line 3 = AGI + PR excluded + Form 2555 lines 45+50 + Form 4563 line 15.

excerpts.excerpt_text: The maximum credit amount of your CTC and ODC may be reduced if ... your modified adjusted gross income (AGI) is more than the amount shown below for your filing status. Married filing jointly–$400,000; All other filing statuses–$200,000.
excerpts.summary_text: Phaseout: $400K MFJ / $200K all other (Single, HOH, MFS, QSS).

excerpts.excerpt_text: If you file Form 2555, you cannot claim the additional child tax credit.
excerpts.summary_text: Form 2555 filers: no ACTC. CTC + ODC still available (with MAGI add-back).

authority_sources.title: Public Law 119-21 §70104 — Extension and Enhancement of Increased Child Tax Credit (One Big Beautiful Bill Act)

excerpts.excerpt_text: Section 24(h) is amended— (1) in paragraph (1), by striking ', and before January 1, 2026',
excerpts.summary_text: Removes sunset on §24(h); makes the expanded CTC structure permanent.

excerpts.excerpt_text: Section 24(h) is amended— (2) in paragraph (2), by striking '$2,000' and inserting '$2,200',
excerpts.summary_text: Per-qualifying-child credit amount raised from $2,000 to $2,200, eff. TY 2025+.

excerpts.excerpt_text: Section 24(h)(7) is amended to read as follows: '(7) SOCIAL SECURITY NUMBER REQUIRED.— (A) IN GENERAL.—No credit shall be allowed under this section to a taxpayer with respect to any qualifying child unless the taxpayer includes on the return of tax for the taxable year— (i) the taxpayer's social security number (or, in the case of a joint return, the social security number of at least 1 spouse), and (ii) the social security number of such qualifying child. (B) SOCIAL SECURITY NUMBER.—For purposes of this paragraph, the term 'social security number' means a social security number issued to an individual by the Social Security Administration, but only if the social security number is issued— (i) to a citizen of the United States or pursuant to subclause (I) (or that portion of subclause (III) that relates to subclause (I)) of section 205(c)(2)(B)(i) of the Social Security Act, and (ii) before the due date for such return.'
excerpts.summary_text: New SSN requirement for taxpayer (MFJ: at least one spouse) AND qualifying child. Work-authorized SSN, issued before due date.

excerpts.excerpt_text: Section 24(i) is amended to read as follows: '(i) INFLATION ADJUSTMENTS.— (1) MAXIMUM AMOUNT OF REFUNDABLE CREDIT.—In the case of a taxable year beginning after 2024, the $1,400 amount in subsection (h)(5) shall be increased by [COLA, base year 2017]. (2) SPECIAL RULE FOR ADJUSTMENT OF CREDIT AMOUNT.—In the case of a taxable year beginning after 2025, the $2,200 amount in subsection (h)(2) shall be increased by [COLA, base year 2024]. (3) ROUNDING.—If any increase under this subsection is not a multiple of $100, such increase shall be rounded to the next lowest multiple of $100.'
excerpts.summary_text: Refundable cap $1,400 indexed for 2025+ (→ $1,700 in 2025). Credit amount $2,200 indexed for 2026+.

excerpts.excerpt_text: The amendments made by this section shall apply to taxable years beginning after December 31, 2024.
excerpts.summary_text: All §70104 amendments effective for TY 2025+. Retroactive to start of 2025 despite July 4, 2025 signing.
